description_long: This is a classic urban fire hydrant with a robust, cylindrical body and a domed top, finished in a weathered, dark red paint. The hydrant features three prominent hose connection outlets: one facing forward and two on either side, each capped with hexagonal nuts. The base and top are flanged, with vertical ridges providing structural reinforcement and a distinctive visual style. The surface shows signs of wear and exposure, with subtle scratches and a matte, slightly rough finish. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible, and the hydrant appears solid and heavy, typical of cast iron construction. The overall design is utilitarian, built to withstand harsh outdoor conditions and frequent use in emergency scenarios.
description: A heavy, dark red fire hydrant with three capped hose outlets and a domed top, showing signs of wear and a matte, slightly rough finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central hose outlet facing forward, flanked by two side outlets and the domed top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one of the lateral hose outlets and the cylindrical body, with the front outlet partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the two side outlets symmetrically, with the central outlet not visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other lateral hose outlet and the cylindrical body, with the front outlet partially visible.
